title: senecio incanus var. parviflorus
intro: Senecio incanus var. parviflorus, also known as the woolly groundsel, is a shrubby perennial herb that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Senecio incanus var. parviflorus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful remedy for various health issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
